scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test mapRow with an extra column ('Modifications') and multiple calls to verify deduplication in protein_mapping.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Test that the function handles an extra column gracefully and prevents duplicate peptide entries in protein_mapping when called multiple times.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must not crash with extra columns and must deduplicate peptide names in protein_mapping.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Naive implementations may crash due to index out of range or produce duplicate entries.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(
    this_row=row,
    header_dict=header_dict,
    precursors_mapping=precursors_mapping,
    sequences_mapping=sequences_mapping,
    protein_mapping=protein_mapping
)
mapRow(
    this_row=row,
    header_dict=header_dict,
    precursors_mapping=precursors_mapping,
    sequences_mapping=sequences_mapping,
    protein_mapping=protein_mapping
)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a variant where the row contains an extra column not referenced in the problem description.
Scaled executable cases must include a variant where mapRow is called multiple times to check deduplication.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test mapRow when 'Charge' column is missing; charge should default to '0'.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Test that the function correctly defaults charge to '0' when the 'Charge' column is absent.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When 'Charge' is not in header_dict, the function should use '0' as charge state and create keys with '/0'.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If the function assumes 'Charge' exists, it will raise KeyError or produce incorrect keys.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(
    this_row=row,
    header_dict=header_dict,
    precursors_mapping=precursors_mapping,
    sequences_mapping=sequences_mapping,
    protein_mapping=protein_mapping
)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a variant where the 'Charge' column is missing.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test mapRow when 'ProteinName' column is missing; protein_mapping should remain empty.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Test that the function does not attempt to update protein_mapping when 'ProteinName' column is absent.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When 'ProteinName' is not in header_dict, the protein_mapping should remain unchanged (empty dict).
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If the function assumes 'ProteinName' exists, it will raise KeyError or try to access a non-existent index.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(
    this_row=row,
    header_dict=header_dict,
    precursors_mapping=precursors_mapping,
    sequences_mapping=sequences_mapping,
    protein_mapping=protein_mapping
)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a variant where the 'ProteinName' column is missing.
